rxn_class_summary: Electrocatalytic intramolecular olefin hydroamidation of N-aryl amides via ferrocene-mediated anodic generation of amidyl radicals
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of ferrocene (Fc) to ferrocenium (Fc+), which then oxidizes the deprotonated anilide (amide conjugate base) by outer-sphere SET to give an amidyl radical; cathodic reduction of MeOH generates H2 and MeO- which deprotonates the N-H amide

key_intermediates.name: anilide conjugate base (deprotonated N-aryl amide, I)
carbon-centered radical from 5-exo cyclization onto tethered olefin (III)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Fc to Fc+ while cathode reduces MeOH to H2 and MeO-; MeO- deprotonates the N-H anilide, and Fc+ then SET-oxidizes the resulting amidate to an amidyl radical (regenerating Fc). The amidyl radical cyclizes onto the tethered olefin, and the resulting C-radical abstracts H from 1,4-cyclohexadiene to give the hydroamidation product.
rate_determining_step: Single-electron transfer from anilide conjugate base to Fc+ (facilitated by less polar mixed THF/MeOH solvent that destabilizes ionic species)

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a with Fc
probe_reagents.observation: In basic MeOH, Ep/2(1a)=+0.83 V vs SCE >> Ep/2(Fc)=+0.37 V, no electron transfer observed; in THF/MeOH (5:1) the potentials converge to within 60 mV (1a +0.61 V; Fc +0.55 V) and the Fc back-scan disappears, indicating efficient SET

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropyl radical clock substrate 17
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolysis gave ring-opened product 18 (67%), supporting a radical (amidyl) intermediate

probe_reagents.name: 1,4-cyclohexadiene (1,4-CHD)
probe_reagents.observation: Serves as H-atom donor to the cyclized C-radical; omission with substrate 15 led to oxidative termination giving indoline 16

probe_reagents.name: Na2CO3 (base substitution control)
probe_reagents.observation: No electron transfer detected when Na2CO3 was used instead of electrogenerated MeO-, indicating in situ MeO- is critical

notes: Solvent polarity is a key design element: switching from MeOH to THF/MeOH lowers substrate oxidation potential and raises Fc oxidation potential, enabling outer-sphere SET. Chemoselectivity (Scheme 2) shows oxidation occurs only on the more electron-rich PMP-carbamate, consistent with an outer-sphere SET mediator mechanism rather than direct electrolysis. With substrates lacking H-atom donor, an oxidative cyclization (C-radical further oxidized) pathway operates instead of hydroamidation.
